commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): prevent deploy cascade-cancel and add upload retry (#621)

* fix(ci): prevent deploy cascade-cancel and add upload retry

Production deploys were getting cascade-canceled when multiple PRs merged
in quick succession (cancel-in-progress killed each deploy before it finished).
The final non-web push then skipped the deploy entirely via paths-filter.

Changes:
- Only cancel-in-progress for PR previews, not production deploys
- Add 3-attempt retry loop on vercel deploy upload step
- Use --archive=tgz to bundle uploads (prevents Vercel rate limiting)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): add warning comments to prevent bots removing --archive=tgz

Sentinel PR #616 previously stripped --archive=tgz when fixing quoting,
causing the exact rate-limit issue it was meant to prevent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ci): extract deploy retry loop into reusable script

Deduplicates the 12-line retry loop from both production and preview
deploy steps into .github/scripts/deploy-with-retry.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): add argument validation guard to deploy retry script

Prevents false success when script is invoked without arguments,
as "$@" would expand to an empty command that succeeds silently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): allow env var overrides for retry configuration

Use parameter expansion with defaults so workflows can customize
MAX_ATTEMPTS and BACKOFF_SECONDS without editing the script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: always merge metadata.yaml so server_version is never shadowed

When a support package contains both support_packet.json and metadata.yaml,
the normalizer loaded support_packet.json as Diagnostics and then skipped
metadata.yaml entirely (guarded by 'if len(np.Diagnostics) == 0').
This caused server_version: 10.11.4 in metadata.yaml to be ignored, and
the tool instead picked up a legacy "version" field (e.g. "2.4.0" — a
config schema version) from support_packet.json or config.json.

Fix:
- metadata.yaml is now always read and merged into Diagnostics regardless
  of whether a JSON diagnostics file was already loaded. Its keys overwrite
  conflicting values, so server_version always wins.
- Removed {"version"} and {"Version"} from extractVersionFromDiagnostics —
  these fields hold format/schema versions, not the MM server version.
- server_version is now the first path checked in extractVersionFromDiagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: persist previous groups to Firestore for cross-session history (#222)

* feat: persist previous groups to Firestore for cross-session history

The group algorithm avoids re-grouping players who were together last
time, but this history was stored only in module-level memory and lost
on every restart or page reload.  Store the last computed groups on the
guild Firestore document so both the bot (/wheel) and frontend
(/activity) paths share persistent, per-guild group history.

- Simplify: store previousGroups as flat array instead of redundant
  nested array wrapping

- Add comment on intentionally non-awaited fire-and-forget write

chore: paths-ignore에 .github/** 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(linear): support multiple Linear connections with API key injection (#496)

## Summary
- **Multi-Linear connections**: Users can now add, edit, verify, and
delete multiple Linear API key connections from Settings, each with
independent auto-transition and archive-transition settings per
workspace.
- **LINEAR_API_KEY injection**: When a session is linked to a Linear
issue, the relevant connection's API key is injected as `LINEAR_API_KEY`
into the CLI session environment, enabling Claude Code and Codex to call
the Linear API directly (comment, transition, read).
- **Connection selector**: Home page shows a workspace selector dropdown
(when >1 connection exists) for choosing which Linear workspace to
search issues from.
- **Backward compatible**: Existing single `linearApiKey` in settings
auto-migrates to a "Default" connection on first load. OAuth Agent SDK
remains single-workspace.

## Changes
| Area | Files | What |
|------|-------|------|
| Store | `linear-connections.ts` | New CRUD store for multi-connection
persistence |
| Routes | `linear-connection-routes.ts` | REST API for connection CRUD
+ verify |
| Routes | `linear-routes.ts` | All handlers use
`resolveApiKey(connectionId)` |
| Routes | `routes.ts` | Register new routes + `LINEAR_API_KEY` env
injection |
| Routes | `settings-routes.ts` | Add `linearConnectionCount` to
settings response |
| Schema | `session-linear-issues.ts` | Add optional `connectionId`
field |
| Frontend | `api.ts` | New types + connection CRUD methods +
`connectionId` params |
| Frontend | `LinearSettingsPage.tsx` | Multi-connection list UI with
per-connection settings |
| Frontend | `LinearSection.tsx` | Connection selector dropdown |
| Frontend | `HomePage.tsx` | Pass `linearConnectionId` on session
creation |

## Testing
- All 3461 tests pass, 126 test files
- Typecheck passes
- New tests for `linear-connections.ts`, `linear-connection-routes.ts`
- Updated tests for `linear-routes.test.ts`, `routes.test.ts`,
`LinearSettingsPage.test.tsx`, `LinearSection.test.tsx`,
`HomePage.test.tsx`

## Review provenance
- Implemented by AI agent
- Human review: no

---------

commit.message:
Add Stripe integration: deposits, withdrawals, and webhook handler

- Stripe SDK singleton with PaymentIntent, Customer, and Payout helpers
- Deposit: creates PaymentIntent, returns client_secret, webhook credits balance
- Withdraw: ledger debit + Stripe Payout with automatic rollback on failure
- Webhook handler: signature verification, idempotent via stripe_events table,
  handles payment_intent.succeeded/failed and payout.paid/failed
- Frontend deposit page with Stripe Elements PaymentElement (dark theme)
- Stripe Connect onboarding helper for future payout improvements
- Local dev setup script for Stripe CLI webhook forwarding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: three bugs exposed by real support package run

Bug 1 — Wrong version detection (e.g. "2.4.0" instead of "10.11.4"):
  extractVersionFromConfig had {"version"} and {"Version"} paths that
  match config schema versions in config.json, not the MM server version.
  Fix: check diagnostics/metadata.yaml server_version FIRST (highest
  priority), then fall back to ServiceSettings.Version in config only.
  The generic "version"/"Version" config paths are removed entirely.

Bug 2 — Single-node package with ClusterSettings.Enable=true wrongly
  generated multi-node (2 nodes) topology:
  inferTopology was converting IsCluster=true + NodeCount=1 to NodeCount=2.
  Fix: require sp.Topology.NodeCount > 1 (from actual cluster_info data)
  in addition to IsCluster before triggering multi-node layout.

Bug 3 — "service mattermost-2 depends on undefined service postgres"
  when database is mysql:
  mattermostService() hardcoded "postgres" in depends_on and hardcoded
  postgres driver/DSN as env var defaults regardless of the plan DB type.
  Fix: derive dbServiceName, dbDriver, and dbDatasource from
  p.Services.Database.Type and use them in the generated YAML.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Protect PuA/HLHS vessel labels from atlas correction (Fixes 1-5)

Problem: pipeline improvements on VSD/TGA cases were cancelled by Dice drops
on PuA cases (ct_1105: PA -0.22, ct_1050: PA -0.13). AO/PA fusion in PuA
is anatomically expected — atlas from another PuA patient has different
fusion patterns, making reassignment unreliable.

- atlas_pipeline: compute protected_labels=[6,7] for diseases with
  skip_ao_pa_correction=True (PuA, HLHS); wire through to all three
  correction steps; lower max_reassign_fraction default 0.20 → 0.15
- label_correction: add protected_labels param — skip IoC reassignment
  when source label is protected; also refuse reassignment TO a protected
  label from any non-protected fragment
- adjacency_correction: add protected_labels param — skip non-dominant
  components and chain-detection steps for protected labels
- boundary_refinement: add protected_labels param — skip label pairs
  where either label is protected
- notebooks/atlas_comparison: add PuA vs non-PuA stratification cell
  after summary table to show wins/losses split by disease group
